actionable_feedback: Add a verification sheet (named 'Verification') that lists each key derived result with the exact source cell citations (e.g., "Run2 yield = 75% (Raw Inputs!B4); formula: Calculations!C7 = ...").
Ensure at least one derived metric shows its formula in a visible cell (not as pasted value) so automated checkers can inspect formulas.

Export/save the workbook in standard .xlsx format without environment-specific features (avoid vendor-locked components). If using a build script, ensure the export uses conservative spreadsheet features that openpyxl can parse.
Include a small CSV or a plain-text summary file of critical computed cells (e.g., calculations_snapshot.csv) alongside the workbook for graders that cannot open the xlsx programmatically.
Update the final_message.txt to explicitly call out the Verification sheet and list a few example cell references that support the 'best_follow_up_candidate' claim.
Re-run the artifact preview step to confirm automated graders can open the workbook; capture any export logs showing success so verifiers can confirm parseability.
feedback_summary: The deliverable artifact (impurity_trend_report.xlsx) and supporting outputs are present and follow the required multi-sheet structure. Deterministic precheck passed, but the model grader could not programmatically open the workbook (openpyxl unavailable in the grader environment), so cell-level verification of auditable formulas and exact in-sheet citations could not be confirmed. Semantic score was reduced to 0.7 for this uncertainty. To ensure full credit and reproducible auditing, add explicit in-sheet provenance and ensure the workbook is programmatically parseable in common environments.
